title: argophyllaceae
intro: The Argophyllaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of plants that are closely related and share similar characteristics. Argophyllaceae is a family of flowering plants that are primarily found in Australia and New Zealand, and are known for their small, white flowers and leathery leaves. These plants are often shrubs or small trees, and are typically found in coastal or mountainous regions. The Argophyllaceae family is relatively small, containing only a few genera, and is of interest to botanists and horticulturists due to its unique characteristics and adaptations.
